business.headline: CNG prices hiked by Rs 2 per kg in Mumbai, Thane, Navi MumbaiThis is the second hike in less than a month, following an earlier revision on May 14. The cumulative Rs 4 rise is expected to impact daily commuters, taxi operators and commercial vehicle users who rely heavily on CNG.

Illness, insurance and the cost of timingWhat happens when a chronic illness arrives before health insurance? One Multiple Sclerosis patient's journey highlights the challenges of getting coverage for pre-existing diseases in India.

Will Kwality Wall's big dairy push help it compete better in India?The company is making changes across products, pricing, factories and supply systems. Around half of Kwality Wall's products are expected to be dairy-based this year, while most of the shift will be completed next year.

Could Delhi HC's Hindware verdict reshape Google Ads and digital marketing in India?The Delhi High Court has ruled that Google cannot allow rival companies to bid on the trademarked keyword "HINDWARE" through its advertising platform.

Sensex, Nifty crash over inflation concern on poor rain forecastThe S&P BSE Sensex plunged 1,092.06 points, or 1.44%, to close at 74,775.74, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 359.40 points, or 1.50%, to settle at 23,547.75.

Absolut Vodka maker battles Delhi ban as Rs 3,000 crore tax dispute deepensThe Delhi High Court has rejected Pernod Ricard's plea to resume liquor sales in the capital. The order adds to a separate customs dispute that has put nearly Rs 3,000 crore at stake.

How Gen Z and women buyers are turning India into the 2nd-largest diamond marketWhat if diamonds were no longer saved for "special occasions"? Across India, Gen Z shoppers and self-buying women are increasingly choosing diamonds as everyday luxury, a shift that is helping the country emerge as the world's second-largest diamond market.

Are India's stock investors overtaxed? Vijay Kedia thinks so, offers 3 solutionsVeteran investor Vijay Kedia has urged the Finance Ministry that the country's tax policy should reward long-term wealth creation instead of burdening those who provide patient capital to businesses.

From rising prices to rupee pressure: 5 key takeaways from RBI's annual reportIndia's economy remains strong, but challenges are growing. Slower growth, rising inflation risks and geopolitical tensions may test the economy in the coming months.

A war away, but not far away: RBI's warning for India's economyFor all the talk around India's strong economic growth, resilient consumers and healthy banks, the RBI's latest assessment contains a clear message: the biggest risks to the country's growth story are increasingly coming from outside its borders.

Under-construction or ready-to-move home: Which saves me more money in the long run?Buying a home is exciting, but one question often leaves buyers confused: should you go for a cheaper under-construction property or pay more for a ready-to-move home? Let's have a look.

Inspection Raj in the service of employers evading complianceIn the ninth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how reduced scrutiny in the name of "ease of doing business" may create space for wilful labour law violations.

Why Delta Corp share price crashed 16% todayDelta Corp shares slid 16% after the Supreme Court upheld the government's retrospective 28 per cent GST levy on online gaming companies. The ruling has intensified concerns over large tax demands and compliance costs across the sector.

8th Pay Commission: Pensioners seek option to choose between OPS, NPS, UPSEmployee and pensioner groups have urged the 8th Pay Commission to let government staff choose between OPS, NPS and UPS. The demand has put retirement security and clarity over post-retirement income at the centre of the wider pay panel discussions.

Wipro jumps 5%, lifts IT stocks as AI deal sparks sector rallyWipro shares: Wipro stock gained as much as 4.76% during the day to Rs 211.20, while its American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) listed in the US had jumped more than 18% overnight.

IT stocks lead gains as Sensex, Nifty open higher on easing oil pricesThe S&P BSE Sensex rose 98.09 points, or 0.13%, to 75,965.89 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 16.20 points, or 0.07%, to 23,923.35, as of 9:25 am.

SpaceX IPO: How Elon Musk's company is targeting a $1.8 trillion valuationSpaceX is discussing a valuation of about $1.8 trillion and a potential $75 billion raise ahead of its IPO. The filing shows the company is asking investors to see it as an AI infrastructure and space technology business.

Why India may be taking risky bet with $500 billion US dealMarco Rubio said in New Delhi that India had committed to buying $500 billion of American goods over five years. The remark has intensified scrutiny of whether the proposed arrangement offers India enough return.

The hidden salary leak Gen Z didn't notice until bank balances started shrinkingYoung salaried Indians are discovering that small app subscriptions are quietly eating into their monthly income. The online debate has sharpened focus on how auto-debits and convenience spending can distort budgeting.

Is E20 petrol quietly increasing your monthly fuel bill? 5 in 10 owners say yesA LocalCircles survey says many owners of older petrol vehicles have seen lower mileage after the E20 rollout. The findings sharpen consumer concerns over fuel costs, repairs and the shift towards higher ethanol blends.

entertainment.headline: Actor Ajith Kumar's mother dies at 85. CM Vijay, Kamal Haasan, celebs pay tribute Tamil actor Ajith Kumar's mother, Mohini Mani, died at the age of 85 after battling age-related health issues. The film fraternity, political leaders and fans across India have expressed their condolences, honouring her memory.

Obsession takeover: A Rs 7 crore horror film just exposed Bollywood's biggest issue Curry Barker's low-budget horror film Obsession has become Hollywood's biggest ROI story of 2026. Its breakout run has renewed attention on Bollywood's shrinking space for mid-budget theatrical films.

Did Emilia Clarke earn $300,000 per episode on Game of Thrones? Actor reacts Emilia Clarke has challenged long-running claims that the main Game of Thrones cast earned $300,000 per episode. She played Daenerys Targaryen throughout the show's eight-season run.

Censor Board cuts 38 seconds from Obsession despite A rating, horror fans miffed Obsession opened in Indian theatres after the CBFC removed 38 seconds of footage despite an A certificate. The cuts triggered online criticism from viewers who said key plot elements were affected.

The curtain falls: David Dhawan's iconic No 1 Bollywood comedy era ends As David Dhawan calls it a day with Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, we revisit the maestro who turned madcap chaos into pure 90s entertainment — unapologetic, unforgettable, and now, a complex nostalgia.

Khushbu Sundar meets PM Narendra Modi, invites him to daughter Avantika's wedding Khushbu Sundar and Sundar C met Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi to invite him to daughter Avantika's wedding to Shravan Sreenivasan. The BJP member shared the photos on social media.

Ali Fazal's Raakh sets June premiere, new poster hints at dark mystery Actors Ali Fazal and Sonali Bendre's upcoming show Raakh will have a June premiere. Prime Video announced the release date with a gritty new poster.

Ranveer Singh meets Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice in Budapest amid Don 3 row Actor Ranveer Singh's latest photo with Arsenal midfielder Declan Rice in Budapest has sparked buzz online. The actor is currently is making headlines after Don 3 exit.

Varun Dhawan seeks protection of personality rights from Delhi High Court Actor Varun Dhawan has secured interim protection from the Delhi High Court against the misuse of his identity online. The order comes amid rising concerns over deepfakes and unauthorised digital content involving celebrities.

House of the Dragon Season 3 final trailer promises bloodshed and betrayal The final trailer for House of the Dragon season 3 hints at devastating battles, shifting loyalties and major consequences for Westeros as the war for the Iron Throne intensifies.

Shahana Goswami admits to open relationship, multiple partners: Nothing is hidden Actor Shahana Goswami discussed being in an open relationship and why "nothing is hidden" from her partners. The 40-year-old also spoke about love, freedom and her past relationship with actor-model Milind Soman.

Film body calls for industry-wide guidelines amid Ranveer Singh–Don 3 'ban' row FWICE has approached major producer bodies over Ranveer Singh's reported exit from Don 3, proposing an industry-wide SOP to address last-minute actor withdrawals and financial disputes.

Hai Jawani Toh: Why David Dhawan, Vashu Bhagnani are fighting in Rs 400 crore case? Producer Vashu Bhagnani has moved court against filmmaker David Dhawan, Tips Industries Limited and the Tauranis over the alleged unauthorised use of songs from Biwi No. 1 in Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. He has also filed a Rs 400 crore claim against Tips.

Ankita Lokhande faces social media heat over photos in hijab from Abu Dhabi Ankita Lokhande's Abu Dhabi vacation post with husband Vicky Jain drew attention online after she shared photos wearing a hijab. The images sparked debate on social media, with several users reacting and making assumptions about her personal life.

Absolutely rubbish: Ananya Panday's dance mentor reacts to Bharatanatyam backlash Dancer and choreographer Sandip Soparrkar has reacted strongly to the criticism surrounding Ananya Panday's Bharatanatyam fusion performance in Chand Mera Dil, sparking a wider debate around classical dance and cinema.

Navjot Ahuja opens up about love, validation and the making of viral hit Khat Navjot Ahuja spoke about the inspiration behind Khat and his songwriting process. He said he measures success by creating honestly rather than seeking validation or chasing numbers.

Man arrested for planning Islamist attack at Taylor Swift's Vienna show A court in Austria has sentenced a man over the alleged Taylor Swift Vienna concert attack plot, reopening questions about the foiled terror scare that forced thousands of fans to miss the Eras Tour shows.

Deli Boys 2 review: Poorna Jagannathan steals the show again in messy madcapperRelease Date:Thu May 28, 2026 Mir and Raj Dar stumble through new power and dirty money in Deli Boys Season 2. Poorna Jagannathan's Auntie Lucky sharpens the chaos even as the narrative turns uneven.

The Pyramid Scheme: Paramvir Singh Cheema, Ranvir Shorey in a risky game of wealth The trailer of The Pyramid Scheme offers a gripping look into the dark world of pyramid marketing, greed, and the lure of quick money. Starring Paramvir Singh Cheema and Ranvir Shorey, the series follows two men whose ambitions slowly spiral into chaos.

general news.headline: U-turn on green cards? US says 'return home' rule not for all applicantsThe US Department of Homeland Security said most green card applicants can stay in the country while their cases are reviewed. The clarification eased fears after an earlier statement suggested many would have to leave the country.

9 with links to Pak's ISI arrested, had planned terror attacks in Delhi, MumbaiDelhi Police Special Cell arrested nine suspected terrorists allegedly linked to an ISI-backed network and the Mumbai underworld. Investigators say the arrests have opened a wider probe into cross-border handlers, funding and possible targets.

Indian staff at US hotel paid traffickers to have sex with minor on cheap ratesFederal prosecutors said Kavankumar Patel will also serve five years of supervised release after completing his prison term. Authorities added that Patel is in the US illegally and is expected to face deportation proceedings after serving his sentence.

Why Siddaramaiah's dethroning might hit Rahul Gandhi the worstThe Congress high command might have resolved the Karnataka deadlock by asking Siddaramaiah to step down, but the move could backfire for it in the 2028 Assembly polls. The unseating of the veteran OBC leader could also blunt Rahul Gandhi's choicest of political weapons.
Can you prove your Indian citizenship? I'm not sure I canCitizenship of India is a nice thought, a happy notion and a grand legal nuance. You think you have it, but do you, really?

CUET UG 2026 delayed at centres after glitch, NTA shares revised timingCUET UG 2026 exams have been delayed at several centres today after a technical glitch hit the second shift. The reporting time for the afternoon shift is 2:30 pm with the exams to begin at 4 pm now. NTA said the issue was resolved, and compensatory time was given, so candidates were not disadvantaged.

IT manager quits job to become auto-driver, reveals how much she earns in a monthA video captured a woman auto driver describing her shift from IT management, and how much she earned in a month. The exchange struck a chord online for its focus on peace, fulfilment and unconventional success.

UP hikes electricity prices, bills to go up by 10% from JuneUPPCL has imposed an additional fuel surcharge that will raise electricity bills in Uttar Pradesh from June. The move adds to consumer costs even as complaints over power cuts and supply shortages persist.

Obsession takeover: A Rs 7 crore horror film just exposed Bollywood's biggest issueCurry Barker's low-budget horror film Obsession has become Hollywood's biggest ROI story of 2026. Its breakout run has renewed attention on Bollywood's shrinking space for mid-budget theatrical films.

Why Durga Puja in Bengal faces a crisis this yearThe lanes of Kumartuli in Kolkata, the hub of potters who make Durga idols, are at a standstill due to an acute shortage of a specific type of clay used for idol-making. The crisis, along with soaring jute costs, has raised fears over livelihoods and Durga Puja preparations.

'Never seen anything like it': Satellite pics show China's buildup near nuke silosSatellite imagery reviewed by Reuters shows China building launch pads and fortified sites near its north-western missile fields. Analysts said that the network could strengthen Beijing's second-strike capability amid rising US-China tensions.

How Gurindervir took back the title of fastest Indian alive from Animesh KujurGurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur are two of the fastest men in India. The battle between the two is rapidly taking sprinting to new heights in the country.

2026 Honda City facelift review, first drive: Comfort, efficiency and legacyIn a market obsessed with SUVs, can a sedan that built its reputation on refinement, reliability and effortless drivability still make a compelling case for itself in 2026?

Not Shiva-Pashupati on Harappan seal? US historian Audrey Truschke sparks outrageAmerican historian Audrey Truschke challenged the Ministry of Culture's description of the Harappan Pashupati seal as "Shiva-Pashupati". She triggered a row by saying that the figure on the seal was "not Shiva", but the image was "more likely adapted from proto-Elamite iconography". Truschke's remark triggered a wider debate on archaeology, civilisational continuity and who gets to interpret India's ancient past.

776 not enough? Sooryavanshi in tears after RR's IPL 2026 ends in heartbreakVaibhav Sooryavanshi was left in tears after Rajasthan Royals crashed out of IPL 2026 after losing to Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 despite his 96 off 47. The defeat ended a remarkable IPL 2026 campaign in which the 15-year-old finished with 776 runs and the Orange Cap.

India's worst monsoon since 2015 could be here. El Nino puts odds at 60%IMD has put the probability of a deficient 2026 monsoon at 60 per cent, nearly four times the historical odds, as El Nino develops in the Pacific and the Indian Ocean offers no counterbalancing force. The forecast, which has already worsened since April, could make 2026 India's driest monsoon in over a decade.

The curtain falls: David Dhawan's iconic No 1 Bollywood comedy era endsAs David Dhawan calls it a day with Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai, we revisit the maestro who turned madcap chaos into pure 90s entertainment — unapologetic, unforgettable, and now, a complex nostalgia.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Who benefits from selling CBSE's OSM success story?As complaints over CBSE's on-screen marking system mounted, positive endorsements from principals surfaced online. The contrast has sharpened questions over preparedness, transparency and who shapes the reform narrative. More importantly, why does the system need selling at all?

Shahana Goswami admits to open relationship, multiple partners: Nothing is hiddenActor Shahana Goswami discussed being in an open relationship and why "nothing is hidden" from her partners. The 40-year-old also spoke about love, freedom and her past relationship with actor-model Milind Soman.

Diabetes is making your sex life worseHandcrafted ByOur Editors We know diabetes affects the kidneys, heart, eyes, metabolism and hormones. But sexual health is rarely discussed in clinics and OPDs. While India has a high burden of diabetes, the disease is also quietly damaging sexual function in both men and women.

Congress in hectic parleys over new Karnataka govt, MLAs to meet at 4 pm Live UpdatesSiddaramaiah met the Congress leadership in Delhi as the party finalised Karnataka's transition. The talks centred on cabinet formation, community representation and his continuing role in state politics.

Hyderabad educationists hire 'contract killers' at Rs 15 lakh to eliminate lawyerHyderabad Police have arrested several accused, including Congress leader Mujahid Alam Khan, in the murder of advocate Khaja Moizuddin. The case has tied a long-running Waqf property dispute to an alleged contract killing and triggered political fallout.

AAP sweeps Punjab civic polls, BJP, Congress take key municipal corporationsAAP dominated Punjab's civic body polls, sweeping most municipal wards across the state, while the BJP and Congress wrested control of Abohar and Kapurthala from AAP.

sports.headline: WWE star heaps praise on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi despite loss vs GT IPL 2026: Drew McIntyre hailed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after another dazzling Qualifier 2 innings despite Rajasthan Royals' exit. The praise underlined how a 15-year-old's Orange Cap season has pushed him into cricket's global conversation.

Day 7 Live Updates: Naomi Osaka vs Iva Jovic underway on blockbuster day French Open Day 7 Live Updates: Naomi Osaka and Iva Jovic will start off the day's proceedings. Later in the day, Coco Gauff, Aryna Sabalenka, Amanda Anisimova, and Felix Auger-Aliassime will be in action.

Don't forget Vinesh Phogat: Superfan inks wrestler's face before Asian Games trials Vinesh Phogat entered the Asian Games trials after the Supreme Court let a Delhi High Court order stand and fans turned up to back her. Her fight with the federation has become bigger than selection, exposing how returning athletes can still be forced to battle the system first.

Day 7 schedule: Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff eye prequarters berth Day 7 at the French Open 2026 features a star-studded third-round schedule, with Aryna Sabalenka and Coco Gauff leading the charge as they aim for pre-quarterfinal spots at Roland Garros.

Barcelona shut down critics, seal 80 million euro Anthony Gordan transfer Barcelona unveiled Anthony Gordon after sealing his big-money move from Newcastle United. The transfer underlines their push for more attacking depth and delivers a childhood ambition the England winger says he never abandoned.

Champions League: Arsenal face PSG with European glory and history on the line Arsenal meet holders PSG in the Champions League final in Budapest after dominant but contrasting runs. A first European crown for Arsenal or a rare successful defence for PSG would make this a defining final.

Djokovic was asked about his future after French Open exit. The Serbian was blunt French Open 2026: 14-time Grand Slam champion Novak Djokovic spoke about his future after a shock third-round exit at Roland Garros, where Brazil's Joao Fonseca defeated him in a five-set, five-hour marathon clash on Centre Court.

How Gurindervir took back the title of fastest Indian alive from Animesh Kujur Gurindervir Singh and Animesh Kujur are two of the fastest men in India. The battle between the two is rapidly taking sprinting to new heights in the country.

Delayed entry, weight change: Vinesh Phogat clears last-minute hurdles for Asian Games trials Vinesh Phogat was finally cleared to compete in the Asian Games 53kg trials on Saturday after days of legal and administrative confusion involving the Wrestling Federation of India.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi more than ready: Sangakkara backs RR starboy for India call-up IPL 2026, GT vs RR: Kumar Sangakkara believes Rajasthan Royals teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for an India call-up after a record-breaking IPL 2026 season in which he scored 776 runs and is the Orange Cap holder.

Arsene Wenger built the dream, can Arteta complete it with Champions League glory? As Arsenal prepare for a defining Champions League final against PSG, the debate around Mikel Arteta's place in the club's history has intensified. Former India international Robin Singh breaks down Arsenal's rebuild, Wenger's enduring legacy, and why this final could become the biggest test of Arteta's project so far.

Play of the Day: Gill, Sudharsan and a reminder that cricket's basics never go out of fashionIPL Qualifier 2, GT vs RR: In an IPL era obsessed with innovation, Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan turned back to cricket's oldest lessons. Their record-breaking partnership against Rajasthan Royals was a reminder that timing, technique and trust in the basics remain the sport's most reliable currency.

IPL 2026: Kumar Sangakkara drops Sam Curran injury truth bomb after RR bow outRajasthan Royals head coach Kumar Sangakkara has raised questions over Sam Curran's IPL 2026 withdrawal after the England all-rounder resumed playing for Surrey, saying the franchise had been informed he was sidelined with a season-ending injury.

Joao Fonseca delivers Novak Djokovic win as perfect birthday gift for momJoao Fonseca came from two sets down to beat Novak Djokovic at the French Open on his mother's birthday. The win sent the 19-year-old into the fourth round of a Grand Slam for the first time.

Who is Joao Fonseca? Teen prodigy from Brazil topples Novak Djokovic in ParisBrazilian teenager Joao Fonseca announced himself on one of tennis's grandest stages on Friday, producing one of the biggest upsets in recent French Open history by knocking out Novak Djokovic in a five-set thriller.

No. 25 still elusive as Fonseca knocks out Novak Djokovic in epic five-setterJoao Fonseca came back from two sets down to beat Novak Djokovic in five sets at the French Open. The result sent the former world No. 1 out and kept his wait for a 25th Grand Slam title alive.

776 not enough? Sooryavanshi in tears after RR's IPL 2026 ends in heartbreakVaibhav Sooryavanshi was left in tears after Rajasthan Royals crashed out of IPL 2026 after losing to Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2 despite his 96 off 47. The defeat ended a remarkable IPL 2026 campaign in which the 15-year-old finished with 776 runs and the Orange Cap.

Shubman Gill 104 ends Sooryavanshi's IPL 2026 party, sets up Gujarat vs RCB finalIPL 2026 Qualifier 2: Shubman Gill's perfectly-timed century and a record-breaking 167-run opening stand with Sai Sudharsan powered Gujarat Titans into the IPL 2026 final, ending Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's magical season in a thrilling New Chandigarh encounter that had everything.

Riyan Parag's chat with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after 96 vs GT goes viralIPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Riyan Parag were seen in an animated dugout chat after the youngster fell for 96 against Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2. The viral moment highlighted Sooryavanshi's frustration at another near-miss and Parag's backing during Rajasthan Royals' playoff run.

Sai Sudharsan's second straight hit-wicket leaves IPL fans stunnedIPL 2026: Sai Sudharsan was hit-wicket again during Gujarat Titans' Qualifier 2 chase against Rajasthan Royals. The repeat dismissal in consecutive knockout matches overshadowed another fine innings and sparked widespread chatter.

Shubman Gill equals Samson with fifth IPL hundred, blows RR away in Qualifier 2Shubman Gill smashed a 47-ball century as Gujarat Titans dominated the chase of 215 against Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier 2 at Mullanpur. The GT captain's fifth IPL hundred drew him level with Sanju Samson on the all-time list and effectively knocked the wind out of Rajasthan's challenge.

Fastest to 1000, 500 in powerplay: Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL records in Qualifier 2IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi may have missed out on another IPL century, but the Rajasthan Royals teenager still rewrote the record books against Gujarat Titans. The 15-year-old became the fastest batter to 1,000 IPL runs and the first player to score 500 powerplay runs in a T20 tournament.

WFI draws line: Vinesh Phogat cleared only for 50kg trialsThe Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) has cleared Vinesh Phogat to compete in the Asian Games selection trials, but only in the 50kg category, adding a fresh layer to the ongoing controversy.

Two days, 2 centuries missed: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi livid with himself after 96 vs GTVaibhav Sooryavanshi missed out on another IPL hundred by just four runs as he delivered another batting masterclass during Qualifier 2 against Gujarat Titans. The RR opener was livid with himself as he made his way off the field after having missed out on a ton against SRH in the Eliminator.

Aditi Chauhan, MM Somaiya to be government observers for Vinesh Phogat's Asiad trialBacked by the Supreme Court, Vinesh Phogat will compete in the Asian Games selection trials on Saturday with IOA and Sports Ministry observers present, ensuring transparency in a closely watched wrestling showdown.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts massive T20 powerplay record in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi added another milestone to his remarkable season by becoming the first batter in T20 history to score 500 runs in the powerplay of a single tournament. The 15-year-old achieved the feat during RR's Qualifier 2 clash against Gujarat Titans.

Why was toss retaken during RR vs GT IPL Qualifier 2? ExplainedA missed call from Riyan Parag forced the toss to be taken again before Rajasthan Royals chose to bat. The muddled start left Shubman Gill irritated and put officiating under an awkward early spotlight.

ISSF World Cup: Aishwary Pratap Tomar disqualified after equipment check failureAishwary Pratap Singh Tomar was disqualified from the ISSF World Cup men's 50m rifle 3 positions final after a post-competition equipment check revealed a measurement discrepancy in his rifle. The decision stood despite an Indian appeal and could potentially dent his chances of securing selection for the Asian Games.

Inside Story: Why Rishabh Pant walked away from LSG captaincyInside story of how growing differences with the team management, a difficult season and concerns over his form culminated in Rishabh Pant stepping down as Lucknow Super Giants captain.

Lakshya Sen follows PV Sindhu out of Singapore Open, falls in quartersLakshya Sen lost a draining Singapore Open quarterfinal to Koki Watanabe after fading in the decider. His exit deepened India's singles disappointment, but the doubles campaign kept India's challenge alive.

Roland Garros under fire after Zeynep Sonmez suffers freak injury on courtZeynep Sonmez retired from her French Open doubles match after colliding with a Lacoste structure behind the baseline. The injury has sharpened criticism of Roland Garros for keeping branding obstacles so close to the court.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
